headline: Moody’s Deepens Microsoft AI Tie Up To Embed Credit Analytics
summary: Moody’s Corporation (NYSE:MCO) is expanding its AI partnership with Microsoft, bringing its decision-grade intelligence into Microsoft 365 Copilot and related AI tools. The integration aims to embed Moody’s credit and risk analytics directly into everyday enterprise workflows. This move is part of a broader effort to digitize and scale credit research capabilities through AI for institutional users. Moody’s, trading at $460.11, sits at the intersection of financial data and enterprise...

headline: Stock Futures Fall as Investor Weigh Up Big Tech Earnings, Iran Fears
summary: Stocks looked set to edge higher on Thursday as investors tried to make sense of a deluge of Big Tech earnings and a sudden jump in oil prices.  S&P 500 futures climbed 0.1% and contracts tied to the tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 added 0.2%.  The three major indexes struggled for direction on Wednesday after the Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged, as was widely expected.

content.title: Stock Futures Fall as Investor Weigh Up Big Tech Earnings, Iran Fears
content.summary: Stocks looked set to edge higher on Thursday as investors tried to make sense of a deluge of Big Tech earnings and a sudden jump in oil prices.  S&P 500 futures climbed 0.1% and contracts tied to the tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 added 0.2%.  The three major indexes struggled for direction on Wednesday after the Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged, as was widely expected.

headline: Google Cloud pulls ahead as Big Tech's AI bet swells to $700 billion
summary: Alphabet's blowout cloud growth has reset expectations across major tech companies, leaving investors to recalibrate which firms are delivering the clearest returns.  Alphabet shares jumped more than 6% in premarket trading on Thursday, while Meta stock fell ‌nearly 9%.  Amazon shares rose 2.6%, while those of Microsoft  dipped 1.8%.

content.title: Google Cloud pulls ahead as Big Tech's AI bet swells to $700 billion
content.summary: Alphabet's blowout cloud growth has reset expectations across major tech companies, leaving investors to recalibrate which firms are delivering the clearest returns. headline: Amazon Stock Bounces Back Following Positive Chip Demand Comments
summary: Amazon said that revenue from its Amazon Web Services cloud unit rose 28% from the prior year to $37.6 billion.  Revenue growth there is key as investors look for proof that companies are seeing returns on their massive artificial intelligence spending.  CEO Andy Jassy noted in the earnings release that it was AWS’s fastest growth in 15 quarters.

content.title: Amazon Stock Bounces Back Following Positive Chip Demand Comments
content.summary: Amazon said that revenue from its Amazon Web Services cloud unit rose 28% from the prior year to $37.6 billion.  Revenue growth there is key as investors look for proof that companies are seeing returns on their massive artificial intelligence spending.  CEO Andy Jassy noted in the earnings release that it was AWS’s fastest growth in 15 quarters.
